name: Daniel Cormier

qualityWinLedger.context: Elite champion-level light heavyweight title challenger.
Repeat win over elite light heavyweight title challenger.
Heavyweight title challenger and dangerous top contender.

primeWindowDetail.endReason: Jones I and Stipe II did not end it because DC recovered to championship/two-division title form. Stipe III ends it.

lossContextEvents.context: Cormier recovered into UFC champion and two-division champion form.
Immediate trilogy title fight kept the window open.
Originally a Jones win; overturned to a no contest and excluded from loss scoring.

shortCase: Cormier is the elite two-division champion case: light heavyweight title wins, heavyweight gold, strong opponent quality, and only losing ground because the Jones rivalry blocks the very top tier.
bestArgument: Cormier's case starts with two-division title value: light heavyweight gold, heavyweight gold, elite opponent quality, and a dense UFC title window.
counterArgument: The counterargument is direct separation. Jones beat him, and Stipe won the trilogy, so Cormier has two major rivalry ceilings in the all-time debate.
whyRankedHere: Cormier lands here because his UFC resume is compact but elite: two-division champion, strong title-fight wins, and high-level wins at both light heavyweight and heavyweight.
whyNotHigher: He does not rank higher because direct rivalry separation blocks him. Jones clearly caps the light heavyweight case, Stipe won the heavyweight trilogy, and the UFC window is not as long as the deeper resumes above him.
finalTakeaway: Cormier is a dense elite-resume case: two UFC belts, major title wins, and all-time skill, held back by direct rivalry ceilings against Jones and Stipe.
